feat(apperror): add standard error types, Wrap variants, and MultiError (#369)

Add PaymentRequired, TooLarge, Gone, NotImplemented, and PreconditionFailed
error codes. Add Wrap variants for all common error codes. Add MultiError
type for collecting multiple field-level validation errors with structured

commit.message:
feat(middleware): add CorrelationID middleware for distributed tracing (#367)

* feat(middleware): add CorrelationID middleware for distributed tracing

Add X-Correlation-ID header propagation middleware that maintains trace
context across service boundaries. Includes context getters/setters and
integration with the existing Logging middleware.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(retry): add DoWithStats result tracking and OnRetry hook

Add DoWithStats that returns a Result with attempt count, wall-clock
duration, and final error. Add OnRetry wrapper that calls a hook before
each retry attempt. Result.OK() reports success.

Includes 11 unit tests, fuzz test, benchmark, 2 godoc examples. 96.4% coverage.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: devaloi <166653705+devaloi@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(middleware): add DetailedLogging middleware for comprehensive request/response logging (#366)

Add DetailedLogging middleware that captures method, path, query string,
status code, duration, response size, remote address, user agent, and
content type. Logs at Info/Warn/Error level based on status code.

commit.message:
feat: implement C bindings for zsys transport module

Add 10 header+source files for the transport C FFI layer:
- http/base.h+c: BaseHttpClient vtable, response/headers types, URL builder, header merger
- http/retry.h+c: Retry policy with exponential backoff, jitter, status checking
- http/client.h+c: Concrete HttpClient with retry, JSON/text/binary helpers, download
- wss/wss.h+c: WebSocket placeholder
- transport.h+c: Module init, backend listing

All compile clean with gcc -std=c11 -Wall -Wextra -pedantic.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: security hardening for findings 2-10 (#87)

* feat: security hardening for findings 2-10

- Fix .gitignore casing for appsettings.development.json
- Restrict Swagger UI to development environment only
- Use constant-time password comparison (CryptographicOperations.FixedTimeEquals)
- Add HTTP security headers middleware (X-Frame-Options, CSP-adjacent, etc.)
- Add same-origin CORS policy
- Replace ex.Message with generic error messages in API responses
- Gate debug endpoints behind admin authentication
- Add rate limiting (5 req/min) on admin login endpoint
- Replace static Dictionary with ConcurrentDictionary + 10-min TTL for OAuth state
- Add OAuthStateTtlTests for state eviction and one-time-use verification

* fix: address PR review feedback

- SecureCompare uses SHA-256 hashes to prevent length-based timing leaks
- Path traversal prevention in GetAdminJsonPath
- Extracted StateTtl constant in DaikinOAuthService
- Added TimeProvider for deterministic TTL testing
- Added TTL expiration and eviction tests with FakeTimeProvider
- Updated test XML doc to match coverage
- Added AddCors/UseCors same-origin CORS policy
- AdminRepository.CheckAdminAccess uses constant-time comparison

* fix: sanitize storage directory to resolve CodeQL path-injection alerts

- Added GetSafeStorageDirectory() rejecting .. traversal sequences
- LoadAdminJson/SaveAdminJson construct paths inline from sanitized dir
- Resolves CodeQL cs/path-injection alerts

* Update Program.cs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address PR review feedback

- CORS: restrict to exact scheme+host+port (not all localhost ports)
- Rate limiter: partition per remote IP instead of global shared limiter
- CSP: skip for /swagger paths so Swagger UI works in development
- Tests: make EvictExpiredStates test async, assert state3 survives eviction with mock
- Plan: update CORS step to reflect actual AddCors/UseCors implementation

* fix: address PR review feedback

- Refactor LoadAdminJson/SaveAdminJson to use GetAdminJsonPath, removing dead code duplication

---------

feat(shutdown): add drain progress tracking, named hooks, and parallel execution (#363)

Enhance Drainer with atomic Active/Completed counters and WaitWithContext
for context-aware drain waiting. Add Hook type for named shutdown callbacks
with AddHook, RunHooks (sequential), and RunHooksParallel (concurrent).

New Drainer API:
- Active() int64: current in-flight count
- Completed() int64: total completed connections
- WaitWithContext(ctx): context-aware wait with timeout support

New Hook API:
- Hook struct with Name and Fn fields
- Config.AddHook for fluent hook registration
- RunHooks for sequential execution with logging
- RunHooksParallel for concurrent execution

Includes 27 tests, fuzz tests, godoc examples. 100% coverage.

Co-authored-by: Jordan <jordan@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add functionality to enable star tracker constraints to be included in the scheduler (#27)

* Add pre and post sequence overhead parameters to integration calculations (VDA/NIRDA)

* Add pre and post sequence overhead parameters to integration calculations (VDA/NIRDA)

* feat: roll-aware visibility with star-tracker earth-limb keep-outs

- Add solar power fraction helpers and roll sweep functions to roll.py
- ScheduleProcessor sweeps candidate rolls (0-360° in roll_step increments)
  filtered by min_power_frac, selecting the roll that maximises visible time
- Roll sweep only activates when st_* constraints are explicitly set;
  vanilla ScheduleProcessor(tle1, tle2) behaves as before
- Fix atomic extend/shrink in _fix_visibility to prevent overlaps when
  remaining duration < min_sequence_duration
- Thread precomputed rolls through _fix_visibility and validate_visibility
- Update pyproject.toml deps to match pandoravisibility (notebook ^7.5.4,
  black ^26.1.0)
- Add test_gap_filling.py (18 tests) and roll sweep tests in test_roll.py
- Add roll-aware-visibility-example.ipynb demo notebook

* docs: fix solar power fraction docstrings — Y is panel hinge axis, not normal

The formula sin(arccos(|dot(Y,sun)|)) is correct and matches
pandoravisibility's get_visibility_best_roll exactly. The docstring
incorrectly stated +Y was the 'solar-panel normal' with Lambert's
cosine law. On Pandora, +Y is the panel hinge axis: power = 1.0
when Y ⊥ Sun (panels face-on), power = 0.0 when Y ∥ Sun (edge-on).

* Update solar panel power fraction threshold and add integration tests for VDA and NIRDA sequences

- Changed the minimum acceptable solar-panel power fraction from 0.8 to 0.7 in the ScheduleProcessor class.
- Added a new integration test file `test_integration_counts.py` to validate the functionality of VDA and NIRDA integration calculations.
- Implemented multiple test cases to ensure correct handling of overheads and frame/integration counts for both VDA and NIRDA sequences.

* fix: correct precomputed_rolls type hints to Dict[str, Optional[float]]

* fix: remove jupyter-contrib-nbextensions (incompatible with notebook v7, breaks mkdocs build)

* Apply suggestion from @Copilot

Breaking up a comment into two lines to meet line length requirements (Black)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* changing min_sequenc_duration

* bumping version

* perf: precompute Sun vectors once per times array in roll sweep

compute_mean_solar_power was called inside the candidate-roll loop
and iterated over time samples calling get_sun() per sample, giving
O(N_candidates x N_times) ephemeris lookups per target.

Fix:
- Add _sun_vectors(times) which calls get_sun() once for the whole
  times array and returns an (N, 3) float64 array via vectorised
  numpy ops (no Python loop).
- Add _mean_solar_power_from_sun_vecs(ra, dec, roll_deg, sun_vecs)
  which computes the mean power fraction from a precomputed sun_vecs
  array using a single batched dot-product (sun_vecs @ y_payload).
- Refactor compute_mean_solar_power to delegate to both helpers,
  removing the per-sample Python loop (public API unchanged).
- find_best_roll_for_target now precomputes sun_vecs = _sun_vectors(times)
  once before the candidate loop and calls _mean_solar_power_from_sun_vecs
  inside, reducing ephemeris cost from O(N_candidates x N_times) to
  O(N_times).

103 tests pass, black/flake8 clean.

* changing default post sequence to 120 s

---------

Co-authored-by: trevorfoote <tof2@cornell.edu>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

10 files, +1,725 / -2,909 lines

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(telegram/nim): Full implementation of client_base.nim

Port of Python client/_base.py (819 lines) to Nim (851 lines).

Implements the complete TdlibClientBase type with:
- Constructor with TdlibConfig, askPhone/askCode/askPass callbacks
- Lifecycle: start, stop, idle, run, connect, disconnect, restart
- Auth: providePhone, provideCode, providePass
- C callback registration with GC-safe closure pinning
- Decorator-style API: onMessageDecorator, onEditedDecorator, onRawDecorator
- Raw invoke: invoke (Future[string]), invokeRaw (Future[JsonNode])
- Helpers: supergroupId, markupDict, fileInput, buildSendReq, ft, formatText, msgFromJson
- Subclass hooks: registerHandlers, onStarted, onStopping, preLoadModules

* Address code review: fix duration arg, NIRDA guard ordering, init validation, and add tests

- _update_payload_parameters_sequence: pass sequence.duration (TimeDelta)
  instead of sequence.duration.to(u.us) so both helpers receive the correct
  type
- _update_NIRDA_integrations: use the duration argument instead of re-reading
  sequence.duration; move missing-params guard before int() conversions so
  VDA-only sequences return cleanly
- __init__: validate overhead parameters are Quantity/TimeDelta with time units,
  raising TypeError for plain numbers and ValueError for wrong-unit Quantities
- tests/test_integration_counts.py: add TestUpdatePayloadParametersSequence
  (8 tests covering overhead-reduces-count and overhead>=duration->0 via
  wrapper) and TestScheduleProcessorOverheadValidation (4 tests)

commit.message:
feat(zig): implement working in-memory storage for all 7 backends

Replace NotSupported stubs with full in-memory implementations using
std.StringHashMap for all storage backends (sqlite, redis, mongodb,
lmdb, duckdb, pickledb, tinydb).

Each backend now has:
- Working get/set/delete/has/count/clear/keys/values/items operations
- JSON serialization via std.json for value storage
- Backend-specific fields matching Python API (db_path, wal_mode,
  host/port, uri/database, map_size, schema_name, auto_save, etc.)
- getCollection(module) for filtered key retrieval by module prefix
- getModules() for listing unique module prefixes
- backup() method stub
- 7 test blocks covering all operations

Key format: "module:variable" compound keys matching Python implementations.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: Kotlin 159 files + Go/Rust ALL gaps filled

Kotlin: 159 files, 16,735 LOC across all 14 modules
  telegram 24, core 40, data 20, utils 17, plugins 14, storage 12
  text 6, media 6, crypto 5, log 4, api 4, config 3, control 2, i18n 2
Go: core 42, utils 19, plugins 12, data 20, config 3 — all gaps filled
Rust: core 43, utils 19, plugins 13, data 21, config 3 — all gaps filled

+6,005 lines, 95 files

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add complete Kotlin bindings for all 14 zsys modules (159 files)

Create 1:1 Kotlin binding files matching every Python file across:
- telegram (24 files): TDLib client, Bot API, filters, types, handlers
- crypto (5 files): AES-256-CBC, RSA-OAEP, ECDH+AES-GCM
- utils (17 files): cache, filesystem, git, hash, http, terminal, time
- storage (12 files): SQLite, Redis, MongoDB, DuckDB, LMDB, TinyDB, PickleDB
- log (4 files): BaseLogger, ColorPrinter, C bindings
- text (6 files): HTML/Markdown formatting, parsing, common utils
- media (6 files): audio, video, image, GIF processing
- plugins (14 files): Router, Registry, Loader, Watcher, Context
- data (20 files): ORM models + Pydantic-like schemas
- config (3 files): BaseConfig, constants, env helpers
- control (2 files): process restart/stop/hot-swap
- api (4 files): API adapter, FastAPI base
- i18n (2 files): internationalization with dot-notation keys
- core (40 files): dataclass models, exceptions, interfaces, logging

All files use snake_case function names matching Python API.
Pure Kotlin with JNA for native C library bindings.
Old monolithic .kt files (Crypto.kt, Storage.kt, etc.) removed.

feat(storage/nim): complete Nim bindings for storage module — 100% Python parity

Add missing types and implementations to achieve full API parity with Python:

base.nim:
- DatabaseError, MigrationError exception types
- Database class with module.variable key pattern (get, set, remove,
  getCollection, getModules, close, transaction, migrate)
- TransactionContext with enter/exit and withDatabaseTransaction template
- BaseStorage abstract class (redis-like flat key-value with TTL):
  connect, disconnect, bsGet, bsSet, bsDelete, exists, bsClear, bsKeys,
  mget, mset, mdelete, expire, ttl, persist, increment, decrement,
  size, info
- MemoryStorage (in-memory BaseStorage with lazy TTL cleanup)
- Glob-style matchPattern for key filtering

Backend files (sqlite, pickledb, tinydb, duckdb, lmdb, mongodb, redis):
- backup(targetPath) — real impl for file-based backends, stub for others
- getModules() — extract unique module prefixes from stored keys
- getCollection(module) — return all key-value pairs for a module

init.nim:
- Full re-export of all modules (base, backends, storage, factory, kv)

feat: complete Go and Rust bindings for full Python parity

Add 65 files across 5 modules (core, utils, plugins, data, config):
- Core Go/Rust: dataclass models, exceptions (13 types), interfaces (8),
  logging, integration, config_base
- Utils Go/Rust: android, cache, decorators, errors, git, http, media,
  message_utils, meta, terminal, text, time, versions
- Plugins Go/Rust: client_setup, context_tdlib, context_base,
  examples (help/ping/say), watcher, loader, modules, registry
- Data Go/Rust: ORM models (bot/chat/client/media_file/message/user/wallet),
  schemas (base/bot/chat/client/common/media/message/user/wallet)
- Config Go/Rust: constants, env

Every Python file now has a 1:1 Go and Rust binding file.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: server-side shutdown watcher via cron (--install-cron)

- Replace direct sudo shutdown SSH calls with a server-side cron watcher
- Add `omega-backup init --install-cron` flag to install the watcher
- Watcher script auto-shuts down server after N minutes of backup inactivity
- Add `shutdown_idle_minutes` config field to ServerConfig (default: 10)
- Remove shutdown_server() from backup and maintenance workflows
- backup/maintain now print informational lockfile status only

- BorgContext: add lock_wait_secs field + with_lock_wait() builder

Making entrypoint.sh self-contained ensures it always runs from /app
regardless of how it's invoked.

commit.message:
feat(metrics): add HTTP middleware for automatic request metrics (#350)

- Add HTTPMiddleware() that auto-registers and tracks http_requests_total
  (counter with method/status labels) and http_request_duration_seconds
  (histogram with default buckets)
- Add 4 new tests covering request counting, duration recording,
  multi-status tracking, and status capturer behavior

Co-authored-by: devaloi <devaloi@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(health): add runtime checkers and configurable timeout handler (#349)

- Add MemoryChecker(maxBytes) to detect heap allocation exceeding threshold
- Add GoroutineChecker(maxGoroutines) to detect goroutine leaks
- Add HandlerWithTimeout() for configurable checker timeout (vs hardcoded 5s)
- Add 4 new tests for runtime checkers

Co-authored-by: devaloi <devaloi@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(sorting): add multi-field sorting helpers with SQL generation (#348)

Add a new sorting package for parsing, validating, and generating
SQL ORDER BY clauses from query parameters:

- Parse() with single (?sort=name&order=asc) and multi-field
  (?sort=name:asc,created_at:desc) formats
- Allowlist-based field validation to prevent SQL injection
- Case-insensitive matching with canonical name output
- OrderByClause()/OrderBySQL() for safe SQL generation
- MaxFields limit to bound multi-sort complexity
- HasField() for checking if a specific field is in the sort

Co-authored-by: devaloi <devaloi@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: sendspin-bt-bridge
repository.description: A Bluetooth bridge for Music Assistant — connects your Bluetooth speakers to the MA Sendspin protocol. Runs as a Docker container, a Home Assistant addon, or a native LXC container on Proxmox VE/OpenWrt

commit.message:
feat(response): add cursor pagination and Link header helpers (#347)

- Add CursorResult[T] and CursorPaginated() for cursor-based pagination
- Add SetLinkHeader() for RFC 5988 Link headers (first/prev/next/last)
- Add 8 new tests covering link generation, cursor responses, and edge cases

Co-authored-by: devaloi <devaloi@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
